mnemonics: UPDATING STATE is like updating your shopping cart: you don't change the existing cart, you create a completely new cart with the updated items.
keyPoints: State in React is immutable; you should never modify it directly
Always use the setState function or state setter from useState to create a new state object
Updating state is asynchronous in class components, but the state setter function from useState triggers a re-render
commonMistakes: Directly mutating state (e.g., state.items.push(newItem) instead of creating a new array)
Trying to use the updated state immediately after setState (due to asynchronous nature)
Forgetting to spread existing state properties when updating nested objects
